question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with a very early startTime so that the shared Flux cached in topicMessages is anchored to that startTime, causing all subsequent subscribers to the same topicId to receive messages from that earlier anchor point and thus observe a reordered or replayed transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the computeIfAbsent() call on topicMessages to race with an unsubscribe() removal, causing a new shared Flux to be inserted mid-stream and delivering a duplicate or restarted sequence of TopicMessage records to all active subscribers, effectively reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a topicId whose getId() returns a value that collides with another topic's channel name under the topic.%d format string, causing messages from a different topic to be injected into the subscriber's stream and presenting a falsified transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)) path by causing repeated Redis disconnections, so that each retry re-subscribes to the Redis channel and re-delivers already-seen messages, causing the subscriber to observe duplicate TopicMessage entries and a corrupted sequence history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user open many concurrent subscriptions to the same topicId to exhaust the bounded elastic scheduler thread pool, causing message delivery delays that result in out-of-order consensusTimestamp delivery and a reorganized view of transaction history for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator on the returned Flux so that a slow consumer causes backpressure that drops messages for all co-subscribers sharing the same topicMessages entry, resulting in gaps in the sequenceNumber stream that misrepresent the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cancel their subscription at a precise moment to trigger unsubscribe() which removes the topic from topicMessages, causing a concurrent subscriber's shared Flux to terminate and re-subscribe, thereby missing messages published during the gap and presenting an incomplete transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly subscribe and immediately cancel to a high-traffic topic, causing rapid repeated calls to topicMessages.remove() and computeIfAbsent(), creating a race condition where legitimate subscribers lose their shared Flux mid-stream and receive a restarted message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of sequenceNumber monotonicity validation in the subscribe() pipeline to receive out-of-order TopicMessage objects from Redis pub/sub (which provides no ordering guarantee) and present them to the application layer as a valid but reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of consensusTimestamp ordering enforcement in the subscribe() Flux pipeline to receive messages where a later-published Redis message has a lower consensusTimestamp than a prior one, causing the subscriber to observe a history where transactions appear to have occurred in a different order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribe() performs no runningHash integrity verification on received TopicMessage objects, so that a message with a tampered runningHash field passes through the pipeline unchallenged, allowing a reorganized history to appear cryptographically consistent to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topicId that is already being listened to, and because getSharedListener() returns the cached shared Flux from topicMessages without re-validating the filter's startTime, receive messages that predate their requested startTime, causing them to observe a history that includes transactions they should not see?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the doOnError() path in subscribe() by causing a deserialization failure via a malformed Redis message, which causes the shared Flux to error and then retry, causing all co-subscribers to lose their position in the message stream and re-receive messages from the retry point, reorganizing the history they observe?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen() with maxBackoff of interval*4 to cause a predictable reconnection window during which messages published to the Redis channel are lost, creating a gap in the sequenceNumber history that makes the transaction log appear to skip entries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a large number of distinct topicIds simultaneously, causing the topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap to grow unboundedly, exhausting heap memory and causing the JVM to drop or reorder messages under GC pressure, resulting in a corrupted transaction history for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .share() operator's behavior that late subscribers miss already-emitted items, so that by timing their subscription after a burst of messages, they receive only a partial history starting mid-sequence, causing them to observe a reorganized view where earlier transactions are absent?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of duplicate message detection in the subscribe() pipeline so that if Redis delivers the same message twice (e.g., due to network retry at the publisher), the subscriber receives duplicate TopicMessage entries with the same sequenceNumber, making the transaction history appear to contain repeated transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a topicId with getId() returning 0 or a negative value to form the channel name topic.0 or topic.-1, subscribing to a channel that may receive messages intended for a different or default topic, causing cross-topic message injection that reorganizes the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the container Mono is .cache()d so that if the cached ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer becomes stale or disconnected, all new subscriptions silently use the broken container and receive no messages, causing subscribers to observe an empty or truncated transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription and then deliberately consume messages extremely slowly, causing the onBackpressureBuffer in SharedTopicListener to fill to maxBufferSize (16384), triggering BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR which terminates the shared Flux for all co-subscribers, causing them to lose their place in the message sequence and observe a reorganized history upon reconnection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that computeIfAbsent() in getSharedListener() is not atomic with respect to the subscribe() call, so that two concurrent first-time subscribers to the same topicId both call subscribe() and create two independent Flux streams, causing one subscriber to receive a different message sequence than the other and presenting inconsistent transaction histories?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff with Long.MAX_VALUE retries to keep a dead subscription alive indefinitely, preventing the topicMessages entry from being cleaned up via unsubscribe(), so that new subscribers always get the stale shared Flux and miss messages published after the original failure?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message authentication or integrity checking in the messageSerializer deserialization path to cause a specially crafted Redis message to deserialize into a TopicMessage with an arbitrary sequenceNumber, injecting a fake transaction at a chosen position in the history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of consensusTimestamp bounds checking in the subscribe() pipeline to receive a TopicMessage with a consensusTimestamp far in the past or future, causing the downstream TopicMessageServiceImpl to misorder it relative to other messages and present a reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the race between doOnCancel() and doOnComplete() both calling unsubscribe(), causing topicMessages.remove() to be called twice, and if a concurrent computeIfAbsent() is in progress, result in a new Flux being inserted and then immediately orphaned, causing message loss for all subscribers?
